title: I trawl coastlines to study the impacts of microplastics on marine life
summary: Nature, Published online: 25 August 2025; doi:10.1038/d41586-025-02644-1Biologist Lorenzo Cozzolino examines how these tiny particles are affecting the behaviour and physiology of molluscs, crustaceans and seaweeds.

title: My moonshot to preserve endangered species
summary: Nature, Published online: 25 August 2025; doi:10.1038/d41586-025-02643-2Coral cryobiologist Mary Hagedorn wants to bank biological samples at the frozen lunar south pole.

title: How a self-taught biologist transformed nature writing — and inspired Darwin
summary: Nature, Published online: 25 August 2025; doi:10.1038/d41586-025-02637-0Gilbert White’s journals reveal his intimate relationship with his surroundings — and call attention to changes wreaked by climate change today.

title: Does dark energy spawn from black holes? Could be a bright idea
summary: Nature, Published online: 22 August 2025; doi:10.1038/d41586-025-02720-6Cosmic maps appear consistent with the proposal that black holes could cause the Universe's accelerating expansion.

title: How a fraudulent scientist faked his career and other cautionary tales: Books in brief
summary: Nature, Published online: 22 August 2025; doi:10.1038/d41586-025-02639-yAndrew Robinson reviews five of the best science picks.

title: Glitch cop
summary: Nature, Published online: 22 August 2025; doi:10.1038/d41586-025-02687-4The wrong arm of the law.

title: RFK Jr demanded a vaccine study be retracted — the journal said no
summary: Nature, Published online: 22 August 2025; doi:10.1038/d41586-025-02682-9In a rare move for a US public official, health secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr called for a Danish paper finding no link between aluminium in vaccines and disease to be retracted.

title: US Supreme Court allows NIH to cut $2 billion in research grants
summary: Nature, Published online: 22 August 2025; doi:10.1038/d41586-025-02721-5The decision will hinder lawsuits against grant terminations, legal specialists say.

title: Net zero needs AI — five actions to realize its promise
summary: Nature, Published online: 22 August 2025; doi:10.1038/d41586-025-02641-4Without artificial-intelligence technologies, balancing human-caused greenhouse-gas emissions with carbon removals by 2050 is out of reach. Action in five areas is needed to keep this goal alive.

title: Author Correction: Macrophages excite muscle spindles with glutamate to bolster locomotion
summary: Nature, Published online: 21 August 2025; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09550-6Author Correction: Macrophages excite muscle spindles with glutamate to bolster locomotion

title: Human brain organoids reveal new path for Alzheimer’s drugs
summary: Nature, Published online: 21 August 2025; doi:10.1038/d41586-025-02628-1Organ models with hallmarks of the disease show marked improvement when a protein’s levels are restored.

title: David Nabarro obituary: global-health leader who fought malnutrition, malaria, Ebola and COVID-19
summary: Nature, Published online: 21 August 2025; doi:10.1038/d41586-025-02640-5Persuasive physician who brought research evidence to the heart of global policy decisions.

title: Peer reviewers more likely to approve articles that cite their own work
summary: Nature, Published online: 21 August 2025; doi:10.1038/d41586-025-02547-1Preprint examines how citations can influence the review process.

title: ORCID launched more than a decade ago, but has yet to fulfil its potential
summary: Nature, Published online: 21 August 2025; doi:10.1038/d41586-025-02431-yMany author-identifier profiles are ‘empty’, making it difficult to track who’s publishing what.

title: The brain’s map of the body is surprisingly stable — even after a limb is lost
summary: Nature, Published online: 21 August 2025; doi:10.1038/d41586-025-02686-5Study challenges the textbook idea that the brain region that processes body sensations reorganizes itself after limb amputation.

title: STING induces ZBP1-mediated necroptosis independently of TNFR1 and FADD
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09536-4STING induces ZBP1-mediated necroptosis independently of TNFR1 and FADD

title: Water in the ‘Devil’s Comet’ shares a source with Earth’s oceans
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/d41586-025-02588-6Chemical profiling traces the source of water in one of the brightest known comets to keep returning to the Solar System.

title: The world’s largest methane emitter manages to curb one source
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/d41586-025-02629-0Output of the powerful greenhouse gas by China’s energy sector peaked in 2014, an inventory shows.

title: Fusion energy gets a boost from cold fusion chemistry
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/d41586-025-02683-8Chemists used an electrochemical method to enhance the rates of fusion in a desktop reactor — plus, how microbes give chocolate its taste.

title: When will dengue strike? Outbreaks sync with heat and rain
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/d41586-025-02677-6Analysis uncovers seasonal patterns of the mosquito-borne disease across the Americas, which could anticipate future outbreaks.

title: Structural basis for the dynamic regulation of mTORC1 by amino acids
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09428-7Using cryo-electron microscopy, the interactions of Sestrin2 and CASTOR1 with GATOR2 were resolved, revealing how mTORC1 activation is regulated and how nutrient availability triggers signalling for cellular growth.

title: mAChR4 suppresses liver disease via GAP-induced antimicrobial immunity
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09395-zChronic alcohol use in humans and mice downregulates small intestinal mAChR4 and reduces goblet-cell-associated antigen passage formation, disrupting antimicrobial immunity.

title: Molecular mechanism of ultrafast transport by plasma membrane Ca<sup>2+</sup>-ATPases
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09402-3Structural insights into ultrafast Ca2+ transport by plasma membrane Ca2+-ATPases are provided, highlighting an essential role for PtdIns(4,5)P2 dynamics.

title: Proximity screening greatly enhances electronic quality of graphene
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09386-0Screening by a graphite gate placed at 1 nm proximity to graphene produces transformative improvement in its electronic quality, reducing charge inhomogeneity by two orders of magnitude.

title: TCF1 and LEF1 promote B-1a cell homeostasis and regulatory function
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09421-0The transcription factors TCF1 and LEF1 promote self-renewal and regulatory functions in B-1a cells.

title: Experimental determination of partial charges with electron diffraction
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09405-0An experimental method is used to assign partial charges based on crystal structure determination through electron diffraction, applicable to any crystalline compound.

title: Targeting G1–S-checkpoint-compromised cancers with cyclin A/B RxL inhibitors
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09433-wDual cyclin A/B RxL inhibitors selectively kill small cell lung cancer cells and other cancer cells with high E2F activity.

title: Realization of a doped quantum antiferromagnet in a Rydberg tweezer array
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09377-1A doped quantum antiferromagnet is obtained by using a Rydberg tweezer array comprising three levels encoding spins and holes to implement a tunable model that allows the study of previously inaccessible parameter regimes.

title: Electron flow matching for generative reaction mechanism prediction
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09426-9A new tool based on generative machine learning called FlowER uses flow matching to model reactions as the redistribution of electrons between reactants and products, enabling the enforcement of mass conservation in reaction prediction.

title: A fluorescent-protein spin qubit
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09417-wAn optically addressable fluorescent-protein spin qubit is realized using enhanced yellow fluorescent protein; the qubit can be coherently controlled at liquid-nitrogen temperatures and the spin detected at room temperature in cells.

title: Electrochemical loading enhances deuterium fusion rates in a metal target
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09042-7A benchtop fusion reactor, called the Thunderbird Reactor, is described, showing that electrochemically loading a metal lattice with deuterium could enhance nuclear fusion rates when that metal is also bombarded by deuterium ions.

title: Thymic epithelial cells amplify epigenetic noise to promote immune tolerance
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09424-xThe activity of the tumour-suppressor protein p53 is repressed in the thymus to augment fluctuations in background chromatin accessibility as a means of mediating ectopic gene expression and immune tolerance.

title: Emerging evidence of abrupt changes in the Antarctic environment
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09349-5Abrupt changes are developing across Antarctica’s ice, ocean and biological systems; some of these changes are intensifying faster than equivalent Arctic changes, potentially irreversibly, and their interactions are expected to worsen other impacts across the Antarctic environment and...

title: Engineered yeast provides rare but essential pollen sterols for honeybees
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09431-yGenetic engineering of a yeast strain provides rare but essential pollen sterols for honeybee colonies.

title: Extremely stripped supernova reveals a silicon and sulfur formation site
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09375-3Observations of SN 2021yfj reveal that its progenitor is a massive star stripped down to its O/Si/S core, which remarkably continued to expel vast quantities of silicon-, sulfur-, and argon-rich material before the explosion, informing us that current theories for how stars evolve are...

title: Atomic dynamics of gas-dependent oxide reducibility
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09394-0Environmental transmission electron microscopy reveals distinct atomistic pathways for the reduction of NiO to metallic nickel by CO and H2, with H2 more effective in transforming the entire bulk material.

title: Cancer-induced nerve injury promotes resistance to anti-PD-1 therapy
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09370-8Perineural invasion and cancer-induced nerve injury of tumour-associated nerves are associated with poor response to anti-PD-1 therapy, which can be reversed by combining anti-PD-1 therapy with anti-inflammatory interventions.

title: A novel bacterial protein family that catalyses nitrous oxide reduction
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09401-4Cultivation of tropical soil microorganisms combined with physiological experiments and bioinformatics analyses identify a family of clade III lactonase-type nitrous oxide reductases with low sequence identity but high 3D structural similarity to known nitrous oxide reductases.

title: SLC45A4 is a pain gene encoding a neuronal polyamine transporter
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09326-yThe SLC45A4 gene encodes a neuronal polyamine transporter and is linked to pain response in humans and mice.

title: A missing enzyme-rescue metabolite as cause of a rare skeletal dysplasia
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09397-xIn mammals, the enzyme TGDS produces UDP-4-keto-6-deoxyglucose, which binds to the catalytic pocket of UDP-xylose synthase, thereby regenerating the essential NAD+ cofactor of UDP-xylose synthase in conditions of low NAD+.

title: Quantitative imaging of lipid transport in mammalian cells
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09432-xDirectional, non-vesicular lipid transport is responsible for fast, species-selective lipid sorting into organelle membranes.

title: Discovery of a widespread chemical signalling pathway in the Bacteroidota
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09418-9The N-acyl-cyclolysine system regulates the expression of co-localized operons encoding diverse secreted molecules in Bacteroidota.

title: Flat-panel laser displays through large-scale photonic integrated circuits
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09107-7Photonic integrated circuits allow laser displays to be flattened into a compact form factor while improving efficiency and colour performance.

title: Network synchrony creates neural filters promoting quiescence in Drosophila
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09376-2In Drosophila, coherent slow-wave activity between sleep-promoting and locomotion-promoting neural networks provides a brain state structure that allows for quiescent behaviours.

title: Axonal injury is a targetable driver of glioblastoma progression
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09411-2Axonal injury, induced in the white matter by expansion of early tumour cells, is a key driver of glioblastoma progression.

title: Could these five future agricultural innovations slow down climate change?
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/d41586-025-02321-3From AI-powered bee monitors to genetically modified yeasts that make protein, here are some solutions that are set to revolutionize the future of food.

title: Dying star reveals its inner structure
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/d41586-025-02425-wA star that was stripped of its outer layers shortly before it exploded as a supernova offers astronomers an unprecedented glimpse of a stellar interior.

title: What counts as plagiarism? AI-generated papers pose new risks
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/d41586-025-02616-5Researchers argue over whether ‘novel’ AI-generated works use others’ ideas without credit.

title: Before making a career move, try an experiment
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/d41586-025-02437-6How to use a curiosity-led strategy to find the right role.

title: Securing climate justice in the courtroom
summary: Nature, Published online: 20 August 2025; doi:10.1038/d41586-025-02623-6Advances in climate modelling are clarifying accountability for global warming.

title: [Editorial] RFK Jr and MAHA: dangerous, emboldened, and escalating
summary: The week beginning Aug 4, 2025 was the most dangerous and tragic week of Robert F Kennedy Jr's tenure yet: a fatal CDC shooting by a gunman distrustful of COVID-19 vaccines, the cancellation of US$500 million in mRNA research funding, and his malicious call to retract a peer-reviewed Annals of Internal Medicine paper on vaccine safety. RFK Jr has built an...

title: [Comment] Requiem for mineralocorticoid blockade in maintenance dialysis
summary: Worldwide, 3·9 million individuals have kidney failure requiring maintenance dialysis.1,2 Considerable resources—more than 7% of the Medicare budget in the USA alone—are required to care for this population.3 However, outcomes for these patients remain poor, with mortality rates of 15·7% among patients on dialysis, the majority due to cardiovascular...

title: [Comment] Antisense oligonucleotide-directed liver therapy: a new tool in the MASH armamentarium?
summary: Metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease (MASLD), including metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis (MASH), is the most common aetiology of liver disease worldwide. MASLD is characterised by fat accumulation within hepatocytes and requires the coexistence of at least one cardiometabolic risk factor.1

title: [Comment] The role of community health workers in promoting health equity
summary: The deleterious effects of mental health conditions are often intensified for immigrants and newly arrived individuals, their families, and their communities.1 Although evidence-based mental health interventions such as psychotherapy and pharmacotherapy exist, access remains limited, and available services often lack cultural and linguistic...

title: [Comment] Excess weight loss and safety outcomes from the randomised SADISLEEVE trial
summary: Overweight and obesity accounted for an estimated 4 million deaths and 120 million disability-adjusted life-years worldwide in 2015, representing 7·1% of global deaths and 4·9% of total disability-adjusted life-years from any cause.1 Metabolic-bariatric surgery remains the most effective treatment for obesity, offering durable weight loss and improved life...

title: [Comment] Global cost of silencing science: editors and publishers have a duty to resist
summary: Public trust in scientific integrity is eroded by the politicisation of institutions under Donald Trump's US presidency. The implications extend far beyond American borders, striking at the core of how scientific knowledge is produced, disseminated, and trusted worldwide.

title: [Comment] Plastics in health care: rethinking medical device innovation, use, and disposal for sustainability
summary: Around the world, some 16 billion plastic syringes and 15 billion face masks are thrown away each year.1,2 Unknown in the first half of the 20th century, the global production, circulation, and disposal of single-use medical plastics is now taken for granted as a feature of modern medicine. But the virtues of medical plastics have a dark side: where do all...

title: [World Report] “It's not about us anymore”: after the Le Scouarnec case
summary: Joël Le Scouarnec sexually assaulted hundreds of patients in his care. His victims are now calling for institutional changes in the wake of his conviction. Marianne Guenot reports.

title: [Perspectives] Shakira Choonara: upholding health and rights for young people
summary: “Almost half of the world's population are women, and the latest stats show us that less than 50% of women and girls have power over their health-care decisions”, says Shakira Choonara, a passionate public health practitioner and activist working in the multilateral agency space. Her current focus area is sexual health and reproductive rights (SRHR) and...

title: [Perspectives] Creative challenges to misinformation
summary: Two anatomy books, open at the sections describing female genital anatomy, are currently on display at the State Library of Victoria, Melbourne, VIC, Australia. Both have been defaced. In Quain and Wilson's 1842 Series of Anatomical Plates, a direct source for the first edition of Gray's Anatomy, the page devoted to “Female Organs of Generation” is patched...

title: [Perspectives] Can plastic waste from point-of-care diagnostics be reduced?
summary: On a blustery evening in December, 2022, we—an anthropologist and engineer, both based at UK universities—huddled on a west African beach, behind an ad-hoc windbreak of driftwood and stones, to set alight a nitrocellulose test strip we had extracted from a COVID-19 lateral flow assay (LFA). Over the course of our trip, we had learned of a substantial number...

title: [Obituary] Timothy R B Johnson
summary: Physician and educator, specialising in maternal health. Born on Jan 13, 1950, in Duluth, MN, USA, he died on May 27, 2025, in Ann Arbor, MI, USA, aged 75 years.

title: [Correspondence] Is Gaza still a place for newborn life?
summary: 10 months after a warning that Gaza's maternal and neonatal health sector was sliding from crisis towards collapse,1 fresh field reports reveal an even starker picture. In the first 6 months of 2025, the Palestinian Ministry of Health logged only 17 000 livebirths, a 41% decline from the same period in 2022.2 In addition to pregnancies carried to term, 2600...

title: [Correspondence] Clarity in gender-inclusive language in reproductive health care
summary: Language in reproductive health care, unlike elsewhere, has always defaulted to using the words woman and women due to the historic invisibility of pregnant transgender, non-binary, and gender-diverse service users. However, in the 21st century, there is a growing awareness of how language can marginalise and exclude people and a related opportunity to...

title: [Correspondence] Humility and curiosity in human–AI systems for health care
summary: A woman aged 62 years presents with shortness of breath. Her chest x-ray is interpreted as normal by an artificial intelligence (AI) triage system. The overworked resident, reassured by the system's apparent confidence, discharges her. Days later, she returns in cardiac arrest with missed signs of heart failure. This scenario reflects a systemic breakdown...

title: [Correspondence] Measles in 2025—lessons from the USA, Canada, and Australia
summary: Krutika Kuppalli and Saad B Omer1 and the world media have not drawn attention to the fact that measles control in the USA (the third-largest country in the world by population) has been extraordinarily good since 2000, when measles elimination was declared. Between 2000 and 2024, measles incidence in the USA only exceeded one case per 1 million population...

title: [Correspondence] INTERLACE: not a new standard for cervical cancer chemoradiation
summary: In the prospective EMBRACE-I study, chemoradiotherapy with image-guided adaptive brachytherapy (IGABT) reached unmatched outcomes and has since become the standard treatment for locally advanced cervical cancer.1,2 In 2023, after decades of high-level evidence against additional systemic therapies, the randomised INTERLACE trial was presented as a congress...

title: [Correspondence] INTERLACE: not a new standard for cervical cancer chemoradiation – Authors' reply
summary: We thank Primoz Petric and colleagues for their interest in our trial.1 We congratulate the EMBRACE team on the changes that they have implemented within cervix radiotherapy to improve outcomes.2 We also thank the authors for pointing out that approximately 5% of patients were spared an isolated distant relapse with 6 weeks induction chemotherapy. This...

title: [Correspondence] Progress towards global nutrition targets and future directions
summary: We have examined with considerable interest the comprehensive analysis by the Global Nutrition Target Collaborators1 of the projected progress towards reaching the 2030 global nutrition targets and subsequent forecasts extending to 2050. This investigation, which is rigorous in its methods, is invaluable to our understanding of global nutrition targets.

title: [Correspondence] Progress towards global nutrition targets and future directions
summary: The Global Nutrition Target Collaborators have presented a comprehensive summary of progress towards the 2030 global nutrition targets (GNTs), showing most countries will not meet them.1 From the data provided in the analysis, only 136 (11%) of the 1224 country–target combinations are projected to be reached, highlighting the gap between aspiration and...

title: [Correspondence] Progress towards global nutrition targets and future directions
summary: We commend the effort by the Global Nutrition Target Collaborators in evaluating progress towards the 2030 global nutrition targets (GNTs).1 However, there are several aspects of the analysis that warrant further discussion.

title: [Correspondence] Progress towards global nutrition targets and future directions – Authors' reply
summary: We thank Jianzhong Yin and colleagues, Michael Bode and colleagues, and Qi Chen and Xiaowei Tang for their thoughtful Correspondence in response to our recent publication.1 The discussions, at their core, go beyond reservations about specific metrics and thresholds of the Global Nutrition Targets (GNTs), and question the nature of global targets themselves....

title: [Department of Error] Department of Error

title: [Articles] Safety and efficacy of steroidal mineralocorticoid receptor antagonists in patients with kidney failure requiring dialysis: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomised controlled trials
summary: Our findings suggest that steroidal mineralocorticoid receptor antagonists have little to no effect on cardiovascular mortality in patients requiring dialysis. There is insufficient information on the effects of steroidal mineralocorticoid receptor antagonists in subgroups of patients requiring dialysis and no information on non-steroidal mineralocorticoid...

title: [Articles] Antisense oligonucleotide DGAT-2 inhibitor, ION224, for metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis (ION224-CS2): results of a 51-week, multicentre, randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled, phase 2 trial
summary: This study provides the first clinical evidence that antisense-mediated inhibition of DGAT2 with ION224 could be a safe and efficacious strategy for the treatment of MASH. The observed histological improvements were independent of changes in bodyweight, suggesting potential to combine with other therapies such as GLP-1 based treatments.

title: [Articles] Building community capacity in mental health care with the Strong Minds–Strong Communities programme: a randomised controlled trial in the USA
summary: The intervention shows that a culturally adapted intervention can improve depression and anxiety symptoms in Black, Latino, and Asian populations and provides an alternative to mental health care shortages by building community capacity.

title: [Articles] Efficacy and safety of single-anastomosis duodeno-ileal bypass with sleeve gastrectomy versus Roux-en-Y gastric bypass in France (SADISLEEVE): results of a randomised, open-label, superiority trial at 2 years of follow-up
summary: SADI-S showed superior weight loss compared with RYGB at 2 years, with a similar safety profile.

title: [Health Policy] Surgical health policy 2025–35: strengthening essential services for tomorrow's needs
summary: Progress towards The Lancet Commission on Global Surgery's 2030 targets has been too slow and too patchy, particularly in low-income and middle-income countries. The unmet need for surgery has continued to grow, reaching at least 160 million operations per year. Ensuring high-quality surgical care remains a crucial global challenge, with 3·5 million adults...

title: [Viewpoint] Bioethics for the planet
summary: Severe threats to the health of humans and other species derive from degradation of Earth's life-support systems, particularly the impacts of climate change. Researchers and practitioners in clinical medicine, public health, global health, and One Health are increasingly focusing on these risks to planetary health, which include (but are not limited to)...
